channel.title: Mahmoud S. Y. Abujadallah — Writing
channel.description: Essays on AI for software engineering, AI coding agents, and large language models.

item.title: Claude Code, Command by Command — Part 2: Context and Memory
item.description: Part 2 of a simple, practical series. Part 1 covered the everyday commands. This one is about the thing that quietly decides whether a session goes well: what Claude knows, and wha…

item.title: Claude Code, Command by Command — Part 1: The Commands You Use Every Day
item.description: A simple, practical series for anyone who installed Claude Code, pressed /, and felt lost. When you press / in Claude Code, around eighty commands show up. That's a lot, and most g…

item.title: Claude Code on Your Phone: A Practical Guide to Remote Control
item.description: Start a coding task at your desk, then keep steering it from the couch — without your code ever touching the cloud. You kick off a long refactor in your terminal. Forty files, mayb…

item.title: Your Claude Code Install Worked. So Why Does Windows Say “claude is not recognized”?
item.description: A short field note on the one Windows quirk that wastes an afternoon — and the one-line fix that ends it. I installed Claude Code on a Windows machine last week. The installer told…

item.title: Why a Two-Word Reply Can Cost 40% of Your AI Budget
item.description: Long chats with AI assistants get expensive fast — and not for the reason most people think. Here is what is actually happening, and how to spend far less. I watched two short mess…

item.title: 39.5 Million Pull Requests: A Dataset for the Age of AI Coding Agents
item.description: A year ago, an AI agent opening a pull request on GitHub was a novelty worth a screenshot. Today it’s infrastructure. Claude Code, Copilot’s coding agent, Devin, Cursor, and others…

item.title: Half of AI-Generated Bug Fixes Get Rejected. We Read 306 of Them to Find Out Why.
item.description: There’s a quiet assumption baked into the current wave of coding agents: point one at a bug, let it open a pull request, and a good chunk of the time you’ll get a mergeable fix. Gi…

item.title: Cursor Is Quietly Becoming an Agent Operating System
item.description: If you’ve been using Cursor as “VS Code with a smarter autocomplete,” the last few weeks have been a signal that the product is heading somewhere very different. Across June 2026,…

item.title: How AI Coding Agents Changed the Way I Build Android Apps
item.description: A practical field guide to Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini in Android Studio, where the real speedups are, and where the hype quietly falls apart. The unit of work shifted from a l…
